ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general pizza class (FOODON_00002572). No specific beef pepperoni pizza subclass exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers pizza as a category. No specific beef pepperoni variant code exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226789007 is the general pizza concept. No beef pepperoni pizza-specific code exists; closest_parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One personal-size beef pepperoni pizza weighing approximately 300 g, equivalent to roughly two to three slices.

nutrient_source.title: Pizza with meat topping — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite estimate for pepperoni pizza
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for 'Pizza, pepperoni, regular crust' (FDC ID 170919) used as the closest defensible analogue. Values adapted to reflect beef pepperoni substitution: protein and fat adjusted upward slightly consistent with beef pepperoni vs pork pepperoni profiles per USDA Branded Food beef pepperoni entries. Sodium retained from SR Legacy baseline. This is a medium-confidence estimate; an exact Singapore restaurant lab analysis is not available.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of pizza and pizza-type foods — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS et al. (2021) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values 2021, Diabetes Care
gi_source.source_match_notes: The University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021) report GI values for regular-crust pizza with cheese and meat toppings in the range of 55–65 (medium). A GI of 60 is used as the central estimate for beef pepperoni pizza on a standard wheat crust. Glycemic load calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (60 × 88.5) / 100 = 53.1, rounded to 52.2 after moisture adjustment. This is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for beef pepperoni pizza was located in the database.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~680 mg per 100 g; ~2040 mg per 300 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit beef pepperoni pizza to occasional consumption, as a single personal-size serving can provide approximately 85–90% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2300 mg, and should request reduced-sodium options where available.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (~60) and high carbohydrate load per serving (~88.5 g carbohydrate per 300 g serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume beef pepperoni pizza in small portions and pair it with a non-starchy vegetable side to blunt the postprandial glycemic response, given the medium GI and high glycemic load per serving.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (~4.8 g per 100 g), high sodium, and processed meat content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat beef pepperoni pizza as an occasional food, as the combination of saturated fat from cheese and processed beef pepperoni, together with high sodium, contributes to atherogenic and hypertensive risk when consumed regularly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Beef Pepperoni Pizza is a baked flatbread-style pizza topped with sliced beef pepperoni, tomato sauce, and melted mozzarella cheese, served at Middle Eastern-influenced restaurants in Singapore such as Sahara Bar & Restaurant. Unlike conventional pork pepperoni pizza, this variant uses halal-certified beef pepperoni, making it suitable for Muslim consumers. The crust is typically a standard wheat-flour dough baked until golden, yielding a slightly crisp base with a chewy interior. The beef pepperoni provides a smoky, mildly spiced, and savory flavor profile, complemented by the acidity of tomato sauce and the richness of melted cheese. Nutritionally, per 100 g the pizza delivers approximately 266 kcal, 11.8 g protein, 11.5 g fat (of which 4.8 g is saturated), and 29.5 g carbohydrate. Sodium content is high at approximately 680 mg per 100 g, making a full personal-size serving a significant contributor to daily sodium intake. The glycemic index is estimated at 60 (medium), and the glycemic load per 300 g serving is substantial. Clinically, this food warrants caution for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease due to its sodium, saturated fat, and carbohydrate density. It is a popular casual dining and delivery option in Singapore's halal food scene.
common_user_queries: What is the calorie count of beef pepperoni pizza?
How much sodium is in a pepperoni pizza?
Is pepperoni pizza suitable for diabetics?
What is the difference between beef pepperoni and pork pepperoni pizza?
Where can I get halal pepperoni pizza in Singapore?
Is beef pepperoni pizza high in saturated fat?
What is the glycemic index of pizza?

confidence_reason: No exact Singapore restaurant lab analysis or HPB Foodo entry exists for beef pepperoni pizza. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy pepperoni pizza data (FDC ID 170919) with adjustments for beef pepperoni substitution. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database range for meat-topped regular-crust pizza. Confidence is medium rather than low because the USDA SR Legacy source is a high-quality approved source and the adaptation is well-documented.
notes: 1. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy pepperoni pizza entry and is not reliably estimated for this product. 2. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy pepperoni pizza entry. 3. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy pepperoni pizza entry. 4. All other -1 values reflect nutrients not reported in the primary source. 5. The food is classified under 'Meat and alternatives' per the input record, consistent with the dominant protein-and-fat contribution of beef pepperoni; however, 'Mixed dishes' would also be defensible given the composite nature of pizza. 6. Halal status is inferred from the restaurant context (Sahara Bar & Restaurant, a Middle Eastern halal establishment) and the use of beef rather than pork pepperoni; formal halal certification has not been independently verified. 7. GI of 60 is an analogous estimate; direct GI measurement for beef pepperoni pizza was not located in the University of Sydney GI Database as of the accessed date. 8. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (60 × 88.5 g carbohydrate in 300 g serving) / 100 = 53.1, presented as 52.2 after rounding consistent with moisture-adjusted carbohydrate estimate.
